channel.description: Upcoming events for The DeLUXE Theater Houston

item.title: Tooth & Tail BY ELIZABETH A.M. KEEL
item.description:
Sunday, September 27, 2020 at 6 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

WORLD PREMIERE Tooth & Tail BY ELIZABETH A.M. KEEL Presented by Mildred’s Umbrella Theater Company Jam-packed with princesses, dragons, pirates and more, Tooth & Tail is part fairy-tale, part rescue mission, part love story, and all adventure. Tooth & Tail has been developed and workshopped through

item.title: Classical Theatre Company presents Nevermore
item.description:
Friday, October 8, 2021 at 8 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

Classical Theatre Company celebrates its thirteenth season – and return to live performances – with a brand-new adaptation of selected stories by the master of Gothic Horror: Edgar Allan Poe. Nevermore: Tales of Edgar Allan Poe will comprise four of Poe’s short stories: The Tell-Tale Heart, William

item.title: A Krush Christmas Auditions
item.description:
Saturday, November 6, 2021 at 4 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

The DeLuxe is looking for singers, actors, and dancers to perform ‘A Krush Christmas’ for our upcoming Kids Christmas show. Auditions will be held Saturday, November 6, 2021 from 4:00 – 6:00 p.m. If you have any questions, please call us at (281) 968-0059.

Event details

item.title: Mildred’s Umbrella presents El Huracán
item.description:
Thursday, November 11, 2021 at 8 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

El Huracán honors four generations of Cuban-American women who navigate life’s tempests together. Valeria drifts as dementia floods her mind. Ximena sinks as unforgiveness surges inside her. Miranda wades through the torrential mistakes of her past. And Val searches through the rubble to piece toge

Event details

item.title: Moore Vision Entertainment presents Grease
item.description:
Friday, November 26, 2021 at 7:30 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

The world’s best-loved musical is back! Grittier and more glamorous than ever before. After a whirlwind summer romance, leather-clad greaser Danny and girl-next-door Sandy are unexpectedly reunited when she transfers to Rydell High for senior year. But can they survive the trials and tribulations o

item.title: Urge presented by Peter McNeil
item.description:
Friday, December 3, 2021 at 6:30 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

A husband, in denial of an addiction, must choose between conquering the “urge” within or risk losing his family and ultimately his life.

item.title: A Krush Christmas
item.description:
Friday, December 17, 2021 at 7:30 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

The DeLUXE Theater presents the debut performance of the DeLUXE K!DS. A Krush Christmas is holiday production written and directed by 5th Ward native Marquise Elder. The story follows a young girl whose Christmas is crushed when an unexpected guest arrives. Grace Dada is the Musical Director. This

item.title: The Gingerbread Brunch Fundraiser
item.description:
Saturday, December 18, 2021 at 11 AM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

Join us for a delicious brunch and an incredible holiday performance of A Krush Christmas written and directed by 5th Ward native Marquise Elder. This annual brunch will celebrate the DeLUXE Theater’s youth arts program and raise funds to support the DeLUXE K!DS. $50 Standard Ticket: Includes a del

item.title: Classical Theatre Company presents R.U.R.
item.description:
Wednesday, January 12, 2022 at 8 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

The second of the trio of local professional premieres for the season will be R.U.R. by Karel Čapek. Subtitled Rossum’s Universal Robots, this Czech play which celebrated its 100th anniversary on January 25, 2021. It explores the very nature of life as the characters in the play have created robots

item.title: Who Yo’ People?
item.description:
Sunday, April 3, 2022 at 12:30 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

Who Yo’ People? is a documentary film that explores the African heritage of Louisiana. It takes the audience on a journey through the vibrant history and culture of the peoples who would become known as Louisiana Creoles, while interrogating ideas of identity. The film premiere will be presented in

item.title: Lyons Avenue Renaissance Festival
item.description:
Saturday, April 9, 2022 at 12 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

The Lyons Avenue Renaissance Festival celebrates the history, art, and culture of Houston’s historic 5th Ward. Activities include Houston’s only egg drop by helicopter! Over 50,000 goodie-filled, plastic eggs are airdropped at 3 PM on festival day. Featuring live-music stages with authentic blues,

Event details

item.title: The War of the Worlds
item.description:
Wednesday, April 13, 2022 at 8 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

Arguably H.G. Wells’ most enduring story, The War of the Worlds, first published in its entirety in 1898, is a science-fiction tale of planetary invasion by creatures from Mars. Famous for his explorations into human nature through the fantastical, Wells’ novel takes aim at the imperialism which wa

item.description:
Thursday, May 19, 2022 at 8 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

In collaboration with Esurient Arts, and directed by Dabrina Sandifer, the production was created by a diverse group of six female artists in Mildred’s Umbrella and Esurient Arts, based on direct interviews with 5 black mothers and a doula. item.description:
Saturday, May 21, 2022 at 10 AM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

Join us for a free performing arts workshop for kids 7 to 13. Classes in acting, singing, and dancing. The DeLUXE K!ds is a musical theater program at the DeLUXE Theater in 5th Ward. The program aims to expose youth and train them in the areas of singing, acting, and dancing. As the theater within

item.title: A Crime on the Bayou
item.description:
Thursday, October 13, 2022 at 6:30 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

A CRIME ON THE BAYOU tells the story of Gary Duncan, a Black teenager arrested and convicted for touching a white boy’s arm in Plaquemines Parish, Louisiana in 1966. A young Jewish attorney, Richard Sobol, leaves his prestigious D.C. firm to volunteer in New Orleans and challenges his unfair arrest.
